EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A pilot sighted three funnel clouds about 15 to 25 miles northwest of St. Croix.

The Virgin Islands Daily New reported that East  End of St. Croix was suffering a localized severe drought . According to local farmers this drought compares to the drought of the early 70s. Major impact to cattle farmers because they had to feed cattle, which normally they don't. Accumulated rainfall for St. Croix through 2002 was deficient when compared to the normals for all NWS coop stations.  During the last seven months of the year, only (52-58) percent of normal rainfall was received.

High swells estimated at 10 feet swamped and capsized a ship boat about 12 miles of Sandy Point in St. Croix. Two of their occupants managed to climb on top of the boat and were rescued two days later.  The third person was never found and presumed dead.
A pilot reported two funnel clouds about 25 miles northwest of  St. Croix.  Movement was not determined.
A 39 year old man who was swimming with friends drowned  after he got caught in choppy waters on the island's north coast.
A person in St.  Croix sighted a funnel cloud just east of  St. Croix. Thunderstorms and frequent lightning were observed in the vicinity.

A volcanic eruption occurred over the island of Montserrat  in the Lesser Antilles, southeast of the local region.  Southeast winds carried a thin ash cloud toward the Virgin Islands during the morning. This cloud moved away from the area in the late afternoon and dissipated.

A strong tropical wave caused very heavy rain and strong gusty winds over the Virgin Islands.  This rainfall and wind caused damage to two generators and the power was cut off to all of St. Thomas and St John as well as phone lines.  In St. Croix a transformer blew off in Frederisksted during a heavy downpour and many residents were without power.
